text:
25/10/2023

Dear Dr. Smith,

Re: Mr. John Doe (DOB: 10/03/1958)

Thank you for referring Mr. Doe for his oncological follow-up. As you know, he has a history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Diagnosis of primary lung adenocarcinoma was established in May 2021. The patient underwent a right upper lobectomy on June 10, 2021. Pathological examination of the surgical specimen measured the tumor at 35mm. The surgical specimen from the lobectomy was sent for analysis. Molecular testing showed EGFR mutation status was negative, while PD-L1 expression was high, reported at 80%.

Adjuvant chemotherapy included cisplatin starting July 2021; pemetrexed was administered in combination with cisplatin. Unfortunately, disease progression was identified with the occurrence of a brain metastasis on July 1, 2022. A biopsy of the brain lesion confirmed metastatic origin. He received stereotactic radiation therapy for the brain lesion in July 2022. Following progression, immunotherapy with pembrolizumab was initiated in August 2022.

Today, the patient's weight is recorded as 78 kg and his height is 175 cm. Clinical evaluation indicates a performance status of 1. A CT scan performed today shows stable thoracic disease. The brain MRI from last week confirms no new intracranial lesions. We will continue the current treatment regimen.

Best regards,

Oncology Department
